context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Walker Ridge Block 758

actions_taken: Checklist developed with all pertinent safety steps for running riser
WIMS broken down into stages with second crew member verification
Stop Work Authority Drill program to be developed by OIM
event.summary: On March 21, 2023, at 02:30 hours, a 25-pound riser bolt insert dislodged from a riser flange and fell approximately 75 feet to the rig floor on the drillship Valaris DS-18. The insert passed between two Floorhands and struck an aluminum stairway, damaging two steps. The drill crew was running marine riser joint #36 using the Top Drive when the incident occurred. Investigation revealed that the riser retainer pin was either never installed or fell out during transport, and the required retention cable (sling) was not installed as specified in Step 6, Item 'd' of the WIMS. Personnel failed to stop work immediately after the incident and continued making up riser bolts for several minutes before reporting.
recommendations: Develop checklist with all pertinent safety steps for running riser to be attached to WIMS
Break down long WIMS or repetitive operations into stages
Designate second crew member to verify WIMS steps are being followed
OIM to develop Stop Work Authority Drill program to increase personnel comfort level in stopping jobs when needed

threats.name: Inadequate securing of riser bolt inserts
Failure to install retention devices (retainer pin and sling)
Incomplete review and communication of WIMS procedures

controls.name: Riser retainer pin installation
controls.barrier_role: Physical restraint to prevent bolt insert dislodgement
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical barrier designed into equipment

supporting_text: The riser retainer pin used to hold the riser bolt insert in place was either never installed or it fell out on the transport to the drill floor

controls.name: Retention cable (sling) installation on riser bolt inserts
controls.barrier_role: Secondary restraint system to secure bolt inserts to flange
controls.lod_basis: Secondary/backup physical barrier specified in procedures

supporting_text: In Step 6, Item 'd' of the WIMS states, 'Confirm inserts are secured with sling.' In this case, the sling is a retention cable secondary to the retaining pin which is weaved in and out of each riser bolt insert to secure them to the flange and was not installed at the time of the incident

controls.name: Subsea Engineer inspection of riser bolt inserts
controls.barrier_role: Verification that all six bolt inserts are secured with sling

supporting_text: According to WIMS, the Subsea Engineer or other person assigned by the Sr. Subsea Engineer were to inspect the joint of riser to confirm all six bolt inserts were secured with a sling

controls.name: Pre-tour meeting and WIMS review by Driller
controls.barrier_role: Communication and understanding of procedures before work

supporting_text: On the morning of March 21, 2023, the 'A' drill crew held a pre-tour meeting, and the Driller discussed the topics of running riser with the Top Drive and good communication during the operation
The On Tour Driller and Off Tour Driller did not review the entire WIMS and did not discuss Step 6, Item 'd,' with personnel

controls.name: Work Instruction Management System (WIMS) for running BOP on marine riser
controls.barrier_role: Documented procedures for safe riser running operations

supporting_text: At 00:00 hours, the 'A' drill crew came on tour and held a safety meeting and reviewed the Work Instruction Management System (WIMS) on running Blow Out Preventer (BOP) on marine riser
Furthermore, in Step 6, Item 'd' of the WIMS states, 'Confirm inserts are secured with sling.'
The On Tour Driller and Off Tour Driller did not review the entire WIMS and did not discuss Step 6, Item 'd,' with personnel

controls.name: Third party riser inspection and servicing
controls.barrier_role: Periodic maintenance and verification of riser integrity

supporting_text: The BSEE investigation team reviewed all the provided documentation and found the riser had been recently serviced by a third party in January 2023 as part of the 5-year inspection cycle and had not been used since the inspection

controls.name: Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Personnel empowerment to halt unsafe operations

supporting_text: Neither Floorhand stopped the job, and they continued to make up the riser bolts on the spider
It took several minutes before one of the Floorhands realized they were nearly struck by a 25 pound riser bolt insert
Also, the OIM will be responsible for developing a Stop Work Authority Drill program to increase the personnel's comfort level in stopping the job when needed

controls.name: Incident reporting and escalation
controls.barrier_role: Communication chain to notify management of incidents

supporting_text: It was then reported to the Assistant Driller (AD), who notified the Driller, who then informed the Toolpusher (TP) inside the Driller's cabin
The Senior TP, Offshore Installation Manager (OIM) and the Well Site Leader (WSL) were contacted to investigate the incident and implement a Safety Standdown

controls.name: Personnel positioning and exclusion zones during lifting operations
controls.barrier_role: Physical separation of personnel from dropped object hazard zones

supporting_text: While the joint was suspended from the Top Drive, two Floorhands were standing on the spider and gimbal platform inspecting and lubricating the riser seals and bolts
A third Floorhand was on the top step of the stairs, and a fourth Floorhand was on the bottom step waiting to access the gimbal
It passed between two Floorhands, landing on an aluminum stairway

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
